page: Amazon Web Services g5.8xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 16 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For hardware acceleration, it includes a single NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPU with 22 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage consists of a 900 GB NVMe SSD, and network connectivity delivers a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps under the Nitro hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks highlight a contrast between general-purpose CPU capabilities and GPU-driven workloads. While single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is positioned in the bottom 10%, multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth fall into the middle 50% average. In contrast, the hardware excels in cryptographic operations and memory efficiency, scoring in the top 25% on Passmark CPU encryption and memory tests. The standout performance is in LLM inference, where prompt processing and text generation for small (135M) and medium (7B) models rank in the top 10% tier, and large (70B) model prompt processing reaches the top 25%.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the g5.8xlarge offers a balanced allocation of dedicated AMD EPYC compute cores, local NVMe storage, and Ampere-class GPU acceleration. This resource density makes it a viable option for workloads that require GPU acceleration without the overhead of multi-GPU configurations. The instance is highly suited for graphics-intensive applications, machine learning inference (specifically small-to-medium LLM deployment), and workloads requiring fast local storage and moderate network throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services g5.8xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance designed for graphics-intensive and machine learning workloads. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it features an AMD EPYC 7R32 x86_64 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs running at 3.3 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Acceleration is provided by a single NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPU with 22 GB of VRAM, complemented by a 900 GB local NVMe SSD and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. While single-core CPU benchmarks are relatively low, the instance delivers average multi-core performance and strong cryptographic and memory capabilities. It excels in LLM inference, with prompt processing and text generation for 135M and 7B models ranking in the top 10%. This hardware profile makes the g5.8xlarge highly efficient for machine learning inference, graphics rendering, and workloads requiring local high-speed storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g5.8xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, a 22 GB NVIDIA A10G GPU, and a 900 GB NVMe SSD for machine learning and graphics workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g5.8xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, an NVIDIA A10G GPU, and a 900 GB NVMe SSD.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, local NVMe storage, and NVIDIA Ampere graphics for high-efficiency machine learning inference.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 CPU with 32 dedicated vCPUs at 3.3 GHz
Equipped with one NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPU featuring 22 GB VRAM
Includes 128 GB DDR4 memory and a 900 GB local NVMe SSD
Delivers top-tier performance for small and medium LLM inference
Features 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for machine learning inference and graphics-intensive workloads
